stem: A precision manufacturing study measures bolt diameter for 20 samples to determine if the process variability is within ±0.05mm of the target. The sample standard deviation is 2.16 mm (sample variance = 4.67). Estimate the true population standard deviation of bolt diameter and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Sample SD = 2.16 mm. 95% CI for σ (chi-squared): [1.64, 3.16]. Point estimate: 2.16 mm.

common_failure_modes: Using z-based CI for the mean instead of χ²-based CI for the variance
Reporting the sample SD (2.16) as the population SD without uncertainty
Not recognizing that confidence intervals for variance are asymmetric
